market.advice: Broad, healthy uptrend — VIX 15.9, breadth 68%, only 2 distribution days, 180 names at STRONG BUY. Leadership is unusually wide: regional + money-center banks are breaking out almost as a group, industrials/transports are at new highs, and semis (MU, LRCX) lead tech. With this many breakouts, be selective — favor the un-extended names (RSI < ~68) over the overbought ones, buy strength on pullbacks, and keep 8% stops. Normal position sizing.

picks.thesis: Cleanest setup on the board — rails 0.4% off a new high with RSI only 56.7 (confirmed, not extended), backed by 26% EPS growth. Buyable here.
strengths: At new high, RSI in the sweet spot

picks.thesis: The semis standout — memory upcycle driving explosive growth, RSI 59.9, 3.7% off its high. Size for volatility given the ~$1,039 price.
risks: High price/high beta — sharp drawdowns possible

picks.thesis: My pick to represent the day's strongest theme — regional banks breaking out together (BK, CFG, KEY, RF, ALLY, PNC all at 100). At a new high, RSI 59.6, 43% EPS.
Best entry profile of the consumer names — RSI just 50.6 (lots of room), 2% off its high, +37% EPS / +21% revenue.
Defensive-growth beverage leader making a Relative-Strength new high, 0.5% off its high — low-beta stabilizer with 29% EPS.
strengths: RS line at a new high

picks.thesis: Industrial bellwether at a new high with RS at a high and RSI 57.3 — broad-economy leadership with 30% EPS growth.
strengths: RS at new high, not extended

picks.thesis: Money-center quality at a fresh high (RSI 64.8) — the large-cap complement to the regional-bank move, and widely held here.
Custody-bank leader with the RS line at a new high, right at its 52-week high — clean confirmation of the financials theme.
Logistics bellwether at a new high with RS at a high — transports confirming the industrial leadership.
Insurer at a new high with a calm RSI 53.2 — one of the lowest-RSI breakouts in the financials group, so plenty of room.
Regional bank in the group breakout, 1% off its high with 47% EPS growth.
Hotels at a new high, RSI 63, with 35% EPS growth — travel/leisure strength.
Semi-cap equipment at a new high with RS at a high — confirms semis leadership alongside MU.
strengths: At new high, RS at high

picks.thesis: Marriott at/near a new high with RS at a high — the second travel name confirming consumer-services strength.
Aerospace components at a new high with 71% EPS growth — a standout grower in the industrial complex.
Utility at a new high (held by a user) — steady, defensive leadership that balances a momentum-heavy book.
Industrial automation, ~6% off its high with 28% EPS — slightly extended-off but a quality breakout.
Rockwell automation at a new high with RS at a high — automation/reshoring theme.
Super-regional bank, 2% off its high (RSI 69) — part of the group breakout, slightly extended.
Mega-cap quality, 7% off its high with RSI 40 — a constructive pullback in a strong name; add as it reclaims.
risks: 7% off high — wait for the reclaim

picks.thesis: Semis, 4% off its high with RSI 52.9 — room to run; add on strength alongside the MU/LRCX leadership.
Foundry leader, 3.5% off its high — the picks-and-shovels of the AI buildout; held by users here.
Coca-Cola Europacific — strong (68% EPS) but 9% off its high with RSI 68; constructive, not a clean breakout yet.
risks: 9% off high, RSI 68 — wait for the reclaim

picks.thesis: Storage at a new high but RSI 69.6 (extended) — strong momentum; prefer a pullback entry over chasing the high.
Excellent fundamentals and at a new high, but RSI 75.5 is overbought — add only on a pullback to the pivot.
Citi at a new high with RS at a high — strong, but RSI 75.2 is overbought; wait for a pause before adding.
strengths: RS at new high, turnaround traction

picks.thesis: Perfect scan score but ~13% below its high — a base in progress, not a confirmed breakout. Watch for the reclaim.
risks: 13% off high — hasn't broken out yet

picks.thesis: SpaceX — days-old IPO (Nasdaq, June 12), ~$2T. Too new for any technical read; this is a thesis call on Starlink's recurring revenue + launch dominance. High conviction, high volatility — size small.
risks: No trading history; extreme valuation; lock-up overhang
